paper_definition: Adhering to rules, regulations, guidelines, or commonly accepted standards during application use or development.
context_scope: User discussion in issue tracking systems
notes: Defined as a human value theme derived from issue discussions.

paper_definition: Providing enjoyment and satisfaction to users when using the application.
notes: Interpreted as experiential enjoyment rather than technical quality.

paper_definition: Maintaining users’ sense of dignity, respect, and personal worth when interacting with the application.
notes: Often related to how users are treated or represented.

paper_definition: Enabling people from different backgrounds, languages, cultures, or knowledge levels to use the application.
Supporting users’ feeling of being connected to a group or community through the application.
notes: Often observed in messaging or group-related features.

paper_definition: Providing users with options and choices, and avoiding forced dependencies on specific services or workflows.
Reducing dependency on third-party services or external systems.
Considering the monetary or economic cost associated with using the application.
notes: Includes data usage and financial burden.

paper_definition: Protecting users’ personal information and addressing concerns about data collection or tracking.
context_scope: Personal data and information handling discussions
notes: Explicitly framed around personal data protection.

paper_definition: Protecting users from external threats or attacks when using the application.
notes: Focused on personal and application security rather than abstract system safety.
